message:
fix: complete decision type aliases and help strings

Add dec/adr aliases to utils.NormalizeIssueType() so CLI commands
like `bd list --type dec` resolve correctly. Add TypeDecision to
coreWorkTypes so `bd types` lists it. Update remaining help strings
in export, ready, and claude-plugin commands. Add alias test cases.

Co-authored-by: dariye <dariye@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Executed-By: beads/crew/emma
Rig: beads
Role: crew

files.patch:
@@ -5,7 +5,7 @@ argument-hint: [title] [type] [priority]
 
 Create a new beads issue. If arguments are provided:
 - $1: Issue title
-- $2: Issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
+- $2: Issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore, decision)
 - $3: Priority (0-4, where 0=critical, 4=backlog)
 
 If arguments are missing, ask the user for:

@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ List beads issues with optional filtering.
 
 - **--status, -s**: Filter by status (open, in_progress, blocked, closed)
 - **--priority, -p**: Filter by priority (0-4: 0=critical, 1=high, 2=medium, 3=low, 4=backlog)
-- **--type, -t**: Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
+- **--type, -t**: Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore, decision)
 - **--assignee, -a**: Filter by assignee
 - **--label, -l**: Filter by labels (comma-separated, must have ALL labels)
 - **--label-any**: Filter by labels (OR semantics, must have AT LEAST ONE)

@@ -29,7 +29,7 @@ Unlike `bd list`, which requires you to specify which field to search, `bd searc
 
 - **--status, -s**: Filter by status (open, in_progress, blocked, closed)
 - **--assignee, -a**: Filter by assignee
-- **--type, -t**: Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
+- **--type, -t**: Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore, decision)
 - **--label, -l**: Filter by labels (must have ALL specified labels)
 - **--label-any**: Filter by labels (must have AT LEAST ONE)
 - **--limit, -n**: Limit number of results (default: 50)

@@ -658,7 +658,7 @@ func init() {
 	// Filter flags
 	registerPriorityFlag(exportCmd, "")
 	exportCmd.Flags().StringP("assignee", "a", "", "Filter by assignee")
-	exportCmd.Flags().StringP("type", "t", "", "Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore, merge-request, molecule, gate). Aliases: mr→merge-request, feat→feature, mol→molecule")
+	exportCmd.Flags().StringP("type", "t", "", "Filter by type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore, decision, merge-request, molecule, gate). Aliases: mr→merge-request, feat→feature, mol→molecule, dec/adr→decision")
 	exportCmd.Flags().StringSliceP("label", "l", []string{}, "Filter by labels (AND: must have ALL)")
 	exportCmd.Flags().StringSlice("label-any", []string{}, "Filter by labels (OR: must have AT LEAST ONE)")

@@ -382,7 +382,7 @@ func init() {
 	readyCmd.Flags().StringP("sort", "s", "hybrid", "Sort policy: hybrid (default), priority, oldest")
 	readyCmd.Flags().StringSliceP("label", "l", []string{}, "Filter by labels (AND: must have ALL). Can combine with --label-any")
 	readyCmd.Flags().StringSlice("label-any", []string{}, "Filter by labels (OR: must have AT LEAST ONE). Can combine with --label")
-	readyCmd.Flags().StringP("type", "t", "", "Filter by issue type (task, bug, feature, epic, merge-request). Aliases: mr→merge-request, feat→feature, mol→molecule")
+	readyCmd.Flags().StringP("type", "t", "", "Filter by issue type (task, bug, feature, epic, decision, merge-request). Aliases: mr→merge-request, feat→feature, mol→molecule, dec/adr→decision")
 	readyCmd.Flags().String("mol", "", "Filter to steps within a specific molecule")
 	readyCmd.Flags().String("parent", "", "Filter to descendants of this bead/epic")
 	readyCmd.Flags().String("mol-type", "", "Filter by molecule type: swarm, patrol, or work")

@@ -19,6 +19,7 @@ var coreWorkTypes = []struct {
 	{types.TypeFeature, "New feature or enhancement"},
 	{types.TypeChore, "Maintenance or housekeeping"},
 	{types.TypeEpic, "Large body of work spanning multiple issues"},
+	{types.TypeDecision, "Architecture decision record (ADR)"},
 }
 
 var typesCmd = &cobra.Command{
@@ -27,7 +28,7 @@ var typesCmd = &cobra.Command{
 	Short:   "List valid issue types",
 	Long: `List all valid issue types that can be used with bd create --type.
 
-Core work types (bug, task, feature, chore, epic) are always valid.
+Core work types (bug, task, feature, chore, epic, decision) are always valid.
 Additional types require configuration via types.custom in .beads/config.yaml.
